framework_name: Jay Kaeppel - Probability, Volatility, and Timing
source: The Option Trader’s Guide to Probability, Volatility, and Timing (Jay Kaeppel)
core_philosophy: Trade Volatility, Not Direction: Professional options trading is about determining if the market's expectation of future volatility (Implied Volatility) is overpriced or underpriced relative to actual historical movement.
Theoretical Value vs. Market Price: A trader's edge exists only when they sell an option for more than its theoretical value, or buy it for less, based on statistical volatility ranks.

expected_value_probability_analysis.objective: Evaluate if a trade's mathematical expectation is positive by generating a risk curve before execution.

steps.logic: Calculate the exact break-even point (Strike Price minus Premium Collected) and acknowledge the worst-case scenario (unlimited loss down to a stock price of zero).
Calculate the probability of the underlying security remaining above the break-even price at expiration, using the security's historical volatility.

Map the expected return if the underlying stock makes an extreme two-standard-deviation move against the position. Compare the distance to the break-even points against the probability of closing outside those points.

tail_risk_justification_naked_puts.objective: Mathematically justify assuming catastrophic tail risk when selling naked OTM puts where maximum profit is limited to premium collected.

rules.description: Only write naked puts when IV is extremely high.
rules.rationale: Ensures premium collected is historically 'expensive' enough to warrant the risk.

rules.description: Establish a baseline mathematical probability of expiring worthless.
rules.rationale: Guarantees a greater than 50% theoretical probability of profit at initiation.

rules.description: Strict exit strategy to prevent full tail risk realization.
rules.logic: Calculate the price drop required to generate an open loss equal to the maximum profit potential. Place a strict stop-loss near major support levels.

volatility_skew_strike_selection.objective: Scan the shape of the volatility skew to pinpoint relative mispricing and select optimal strikes.
volatility_skew_strike_selection.mapping_the_skew: Determine if there is an upward-sloping or downward-sloping skew (e.g., lower strike prices trading at higher volatilities than ATM or higher strikes).

targeting_strike_disparities.execution: Sell an option trading at a significantly higher implied volatility (expensive) and buy a further OTM option trading at a lower implied volatility (cheap).
targeting_strike_disparities.outcome: Systematically places mathematical odds further in the trader's favor by spreading cheap options against expensive ones.

theta_time_decay.optimal_dte_window: 60 days or fewer until expiration to capitalize on accelerating decay.
theta_time_decay.risk_management: Track the 14-DTE threshold for extreme Gamma risk and assignment potential.

quantitative_greeks_parameters.vega_premium_pricing: Sell premium only when Relative Volatility Rank is 10 to maximize collected time premium.
